results.title: Athletes (Greek)
results.brief_description: The growth of track and field competition in Australia is studied, junior events as well as international competitions. We see the work being done to encourage youngsters to participate in track and field events. The film contains some very excellent slow motion sequences of athletes performing at their peak, which evoke a strange sort of fluid beauty in this otherwise rather harsh world of sweat and strain.

results.title: Wirritt wirritt [Italian version]: an Australian legend
results.creator_credit: Malcolm Otton, Australian National Film Board and Australian news and Information Bureau
results.brief_description: Artist James Cant re-creates, in rock paintings, an ancient Aboriginal legend about the origins of making fire. Narrated by Ray Barrett.

results.title: Great Barrier Reef (Italian)
results.creator_credit: Richard Mason and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: The Great Barrier Reef of Queensland is one of the great reservoirs of marine and animal life. This documentary shows in detail, the birds, fish and mammals of the area and the work being done by scientists in studying them. A long sequence deals with the life cycle of the turtle, another with the microscopic creatures of the sea.

results.title: The Big island [Greek version]
results.creator_credit: Frank Bagnall and Australian Commonwealth Film Unit
results.brief_description: The aim of this film is to show an international audience within a limited time span some attractive aspects of Australian life. Representing Australia as a brisk and exciting place and creating a mood of a young country on the move. Cinematography by Keith Gow and Don McAlpine. Music by George Dreyfus. (Awards: AFI Travel Award 1970)

results.title: Somewhere to go
results.creator_credit: Brian Bergin, John Morris and South Australian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Hugh Hudson, South Australian Minister for Education, discusses the establishment of school-based community centres in two Adelaide suburbs, Thebarton and Angle Park. The surveys and plans, initiated in mid 1973, show that an essential feature of the planning process was the participation of the communities concerned.
results.creator_credit_for_label: Brian Bergin, John Morris and South Australian Film Corporation

results.title: Leisure lines
results.creator_credit: Terry Jennings, New Films and South Australian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Leisure and recreation are becoming increasingly important to people in the community. Its important that there be some physical side to it and also enjoyment and relaxation. In addition, as this film points out, good facilities and community organisations are required to help people make the most of their leisuretime. Produced for the South Australian Department of Tourism, Recreation and Sport.

results.title: Kangaroo Island
results.creator_credit: Ron Lowe and South Australian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: The Australian wildlife seen by visitors to Kangaroo Island, South Australia, is the subject of this award-winning film. Koalas, kangaroos, emus, pelicans and seals abound on this island sanctuary, completely unafraid of humans. Their daily activities form a fascinating study. Contains no commentary, only sounds of nature and background music composed by Bruce Smeaton. Suitable for upper primary and lower secondary levels.

results.title: The Barossa
results.creator_credit: David Lourie, Albert Falzon Productions and South Australian Film Corporation
results.brief_description: Unsurpassed scenery, friendly people, vintage festivals, a pleasant sip of wine and unrivalled cuisine - all are part of the Barossa. This film shows the places, people and traditional events which make the Barossa Valley one of Australia’s most exciting wine districts.
results.creator_credit_for_label: David Lourie, Albert Falzon Productions and South Australian Film Corporation

results.title: Shrine
results.creator_credit: David Bilcock, John Dixon, Kent Chadwick, Cambridge Film Productions and Film Victoria
results.brief_description: The Shrine of Remembrance in Melbourne was built as a memorial to Victorians who served in the first World War, and also honours those who served in the Second World War. The film looks at the Shrine, and the feelings of pride and mateship amongst Australian soldiers who fought in both wars.

results.title: Study of a 2,100 year old corpse
results.brief_description: In 1972 a Chinese archaeological dig excavated the tomb of a woman who had been buried for 2,100 years. The body was so well preserved that many different scientific teams were requested to dissect and examine it to determine what it could reveal about ancient Chinese civilisation. With English commentary and subtitles.
